calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-01-2016 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-22-2016 Continued To Apr-20-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Defendants Levi Strauss & Co., Maryann Nichols And Levi Strauss De Espana, S.A'S Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award And Enter Judgment Thereon
Off Calendar Jan-28-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jotham Stein of Law Offices of Jotham S. Stein P.C. telephone number 630-443-4390 for Plaintiff/Respondent Ganaderia.
Gregory Gilchrist of Kilpatrick Townsend & Stockton LLP telephone number 415-273-7564 for Defendants Levi Struass & Co., Levi Strauss De Espana and Maryann Nichols.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling.
Defendants Levi Strauss & Co., Maryann Nichols And Levi Strauss De Espana, S.A'S Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award And Enter Judgment Thereon - Arbitration award is confirmed. Ganaderia has not stated valid grounds to vacate pursuant to CCP 1286.2. The moving party is to provide a form of order and comply with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ernest Goldsmith. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/EG)

Plaintiff Ganaderia Brasil Industria E Comercio De Acessorio'S Motion For Withdrawal Of Attorney Of Record
Off Calendar Jan-28-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jotham Stein of Law Offices of Jotham S. Stein P.C. telephone number 630-443-4390 for Plaintiff/Respondent Ganaderia.
Gregory Gilchrist of Kilpatrick Townsend & Stockton LLP telephone number 415-273-7564 for Defendants Levi Struass & Co., Levi Strauss De Espana and Maryann Nichols.

The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Attorney Jotham S. Stein's motion to be relieved as counsel for Plaintiff Ganaderia Brasil Industria E Comercio De Acessorio is granted. Order signed in open Court.

Judge: Ernest Goldsmith. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/EG)

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

Judge: Ernest Goldsmith. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Not reported. (302/EG)

MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
Off Calendar Jan-20-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

PLAINTIFF GANADERIA BRASIL INDUSTRIA E COMERCIO DE ACESSORIO'S MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD is continued to January 28, 2016 at 9:30 a.m., in Dept. 302, on the Court's own motion.

Judge: Ernest Goldsmith. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Not reported. (302/EG)

Notice Of Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award And Enter Judgment Thereon
Off Calendar Nov-24-2015 Continued To Jan-20-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302

MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
Off Calendar Nov-24-2015 Continued To Jan-20-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. JOTHAM S. STEIN, ESQ. of Law Offices of Jotham S. Stein P.C., Tel No. 650-327-1900 appearing for the moving party;
No other appearances made in this matter.

The Court indicates it is inclined to reverse its tentative ruling and deny Plaintiff's counsel's motion. Matter argued by Plaintiff's counsel. The Court vacates its tentative ruling, and grants Plaintiff's counsel's request to continue the hearing on the Petition to Confirm Arbitration Award and Enter Judgment Therein (presently set for December 16, 2015) to January 20, 2016 at 9:30 a.m. in Dept. 302, Law & Motion. Court to mail notice to parties. The Court grants Plaintiff's counsel's request to continue Plaintiff's counsel's Motion to Be Relieved As Counsel to January 20, 2016 at 9:30 a.m. in Dept. 302, Law & Motion. Judge: Ernest H. CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-24-2014 Continued To Oct-15-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For A Protective Order Requiring Pltfs To Return Privileged Documents And Prohibiting Futher Use Thereof
Off Calendar Jun-26-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Steven Piser, Esq., 510.835.5582, for plaintiff Ganaderia Brasil Industria E Comercio de Acessorio.
Gregory Gilchrist, Esq., 415.273.7564, and Ryan Bricker, Esq., 415.273.4368, for defendants Levi Strauss & Co. and Maryann Nichols.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANTS LEVI STRAUSS & CO. and MARYANN NICHOLS' motion for a protective order, immediate return of all attorney-client/attorney-attorney work-product privileged documents identified in its privilege log, and prohibition of use of the privileged documents, is granted. The communications in question are unquestionably covered by the attorney client/attorney work product privileges. (Evid. Code 954.) Defendant did not waive said privileges based on the communications with plaintiff's co-counsels during the period December 24, 2013 through February 7, 2014. (Evid. Code 912.) It has long been the rule the "gotcha" theory of waiver as the equivalent of actual waiver is not a voluntary release of privileged documents. (See State Compensation Ins. Fund vs. WPS, Inc., (1999) 70 Cal.App.4th 644.) Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Cathy Meyer #11596, 877.451.1580.

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-26-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jothan Stein, Esq., 650.327.1900, for plaintiff Ganaderia Brasil Industria E Comercio de Acessorio.
Gregory Gilchrist, Esq., 415.273.7564, and Ryan Bricker, Esq., 415.273.4368, for defendants Levi Strauss & Co. and Maryann Nichols.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANTS LEVI STRAUSS & CO. and MARYANN NICHOLS' DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained in part and overruled in part. The Demurrer is overruled as to Defendant Nichols. Individual employees are immune from liability for corporate wrongs only if they do not authorize, direct, or in some meaningful sense actively participate in the wrongful conduct. (Asahi Kasei Pharma Corporation v. Actelion Ltd. (2013) 222 Cal.App.4th 945, 966.) The Demurrer is overruled as to causes of action one, five, and six. Defendants admit that they are not a parties to the contract at issue and do not assert themselves as agents of any contracting party, and they are therefore "strangers" to the contract. (Id. at 963-64.) The Demurrer as to the second, third, and fourth causes of action is sustained with twenty days leave to amend to permit Plaintiff to properly allege that Defendant is either a party to the contract or is liable under the alter ego doctrine. Finally, the Demurrer to the eighth cause of action is overruled as a Complaint may state a cause of action for injunctive relief. In light of the ruling on the Demurrer, the Court need not rule on the petition to compel arbitration and stay the other causes of action. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer #5976.
